section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing.
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents.
Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3.
Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed.
section13_checks.check_notes: Artifact schema_id 'orch.tier5.part_b_assembled_draft.v1' begins with 'orch.tier5.' and is therefore a Phase 8 artifact subject to this check. Direct verification of gate_pass_declaration in budget_gate_assessment.json was not possible under TAPM input boundary constraints (phase_outputs/ is excluded from declared reads). However, the artifact's traceability_footer.primary_sources explicitly lists 'docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json', and consistency check CC-08 ('Budget and resource claim consistency across all three sections') reports status 'consistent' with the same file as a source_ref. No budget figures (€, EUR, person-months as confirmed values) appear in the assembled draft content itself. These converging indicators are consistent with a passed budget gate. No violation evidence found.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4.
section13_checks.check_notes: Not verifiable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs agent execution behavior during workflow execution, not artifact structure or content. Per skill specification, record check_status 'pass' for this non-artifact-checkable prohibition. Enforcement delegated to decision-log-update skill.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic.
section13_checks.check_notes: Not verifiable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs workflow and skill scope expansion at execution time, not deliverable artifact content. Per skill specification, record check_status 'pass' for this non-artifact-checkable prohibition.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete.
section13_checks.check_notes: The artifact's traceability_footer.primary_sources references only lower-numbered phase outputs: phase3_wp_design (Phase 3), phase5_impact_architecture (Phase 5), and phase7_budget_gate (Phase 7). No forward-phase references (i.e., Phase 9 or higher) are present. Section artifact sources reference appropriate Tier 3 and Tier 4 paths. No evidence of phase reordering in the artifact's sourcing or content.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable.
section13_checks.check_notes: Applicable to Tier 5 deliverable artifacts. CC-12 in the consistency_log confirms: 'No section has overall_status=unresolved. The two inferred sections have documented inference chains in their claim_statuses arrays.' All three sections (excellence, impact, implementation) have overall_status of 'inferred' or 'confirmed', not 'unresolved'. Inconsistencies identified in CC-01 (incomplete objective enumeration), CC-04 (D2-02 dual attribution), CC-06 (metric loss), and CC-09 (terminology inconsistency) are all explicitly flagged in the consistency_log with detailed inconsistency_note entries and source_refs — they are not silently absorbed. The assembled draft functions as a consistency-checking layer that surfaces these issues for resolution rather than finalizing text over them. No §13.8 violation.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible.
section13_checks.check_notes: The assembled draft's content consists of a section index (three entries) and twelve cross-section consistency checks (CC-01 through CC-12). None of the consistency findings assert Horizon Europe regulatory requirements, participation conditions, financial regulation rules, or grant agreement terms that would require Tier 1 source references. The single reference to 'CLAUDE.md Section 12.2' in CC-12's source_basis is a reference to the orchestration constitution (not a regulatory claim about Horizon Europe programme rules) and is therefore outside the scope of this check. No §13.9 violation.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4.
section13_checks.check_notes: Applicable to Tier 5 artifacts. The artifact's traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is false, triggering examination of validation_status.claim_statuses[]. The assembled draft does not contain a validation_status.claim_statuses[] field; the derivation_note explains: 'Assembled draft inherits section-level proposal claim traceability from referenced section artifacts; this artifact adds only section index and cross-section consistency findings.' The consistency checks CC-01 through CC-12 that constitute the artifact's own content all carry source_refs pointing to Tier 3 paths (docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json, outcomes.json, impacts.json; docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json) and Tier 4 paths (phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design, phase5_impact_architecture, phase7_budget_gate). Per check logic: 'For each unresolved claim with no source_ref in traceability_footer: violation.' With no claim_statuses[] entries, there are no unresolved claims without source_refs. The false declaration reflects honest self-reporting of inherited traceability, not a violation. No §13.10 violation detected. Note: verification of full section-level traceability requires auditing the individual section artifacts separately.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions.
section13_checks.check_notes: Not verifiable from a deliverable artifact alone. This prohibition governs the integrity of Tier 1 and Tier 2 source documents, not deliverable artifact content. Per skill specification, record check_status 'pass' for this non-artifact-checkable prohibition.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional.
section13_checks.check_notes: Not directly verifiable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs agent compliance disposition at execution time. Per skill specification, record check_status 'pass' for this non-artifact-checkable prohibition.
